title: Upload Image execute race: setOutput via useEffect lags behind execute return
description: The new dual-upload Upload Image emits its ImageRef value via a useEffect that depends on [files, uploads, setOutput]. When the user runs the pipeline, upload_image's execute awaits the upload promises and returns; the pipeline runner immediately resolves freshInputs for the next block from blockStatesRef. But setOutput is only called by the useEffect AFTER React re-renders with the new uploads state — which happens after execute has already returned. Result: downstream blocks see stale/missing inputs.image and fail with 'Image URL is required'. Fix: have execute build the final ImageRef payload from the resolved upload URLs and call setOutput synchronously before returning.

title: Settings page + named endpoint registry
description: Foundational. Global Settings page in BlockFlow UI holding all credentials (RunPod API key, R2 creds, OpenRouter key, CivitAI key) and endpoint references (ComfyGen, AIO trainer, ComfyGen network volume ID). Blocks reference endpoints by name from a dropdown. Persistent backing store (sqlite alongside run_history.db or JSON). Most other work streams depend on this. Needs grilling: schema, settings persistence layer, named-vs-inline UX, migration path for existing inline-config blocks.
design:
## UI placement + structure (post-deep-grill)

**Route:** `/settings` — new Next.js page at `frontend/src/app/settings/page.tsx`
**Nav entry:** gear icon on the right side of NavBar (not in the main `Generate | Artifacts` strip — Settings is meta, not a creative destination)
**Page layout:** in-page sidebar nav (4 sections) + main pane

## Section contents

### Tab 1 — Credentials
All fields: input with show/hide toggle + "Validate" button (async; never blocks save per D3).

| Field | Used by |
|---|---|
| RunPod API key | All endpoint provisioning + job submission |
| R2: endpoint URL, access key ID, secret, bucket | LoRA + dataset transport (.6) |
| OpenRouter API key | `prompt_writer`, `i2v_prompt_writer` |
| CivitAI API key | `civitai_share` |
| HuggingFace token | Preset installs with `hf_token_required` (.3) |
| imgbb API key | `upload_image_to_tmpfiles` (imgbb upload path) |
| tmpfiles API key | `upload_image_to_tmpfiles` (authenticated tier) |

### Tab 2 — Endpoints
One row per type. Single endpoint each (D3).

| Row | Fields | Actions |
|---|---|---|
| ComfyGen | endpoint ID, network volume ID, status, GPU tier, volume size, provisioned date | Set up (→ wizard), Health-check, Tear down + recreate |
| AIO LoRA trainer | endpoint ID, status, GPU tier, volume size, provisioned date | Set up (→ wizard), Health-check, Tear down + recreate |

### Tab 3 — Storage
- ComfyGen volume usage bar (used / available, fetched live from the endpoint)
- Installed presets table: name, version, install date, disk usage, last used. Per-row Uninstall (blocked while in use by an active pipeline; tooltip explains)
- "Browse marketplace" button → navigates to `/presets`

### Tab 4 — App
- BlockFlow version
- Default output directory (path picker; defaults to `./output` relative to BlockFlow cwd)
- Run history retention: dropdown (30 days / 90 days / 1 year / Forever); default 90 days; background prune job
- Links: GitHub repo, /docs, GitHub Discussions, ComfyGen project
- MIT license link

### What's NOT in Settings (stays in-block, as today)
- `prompt_writer` / `i2v_prompt_writer` model preferences (stored in `prompt_writer_settings.json`)
- `image_upscale` / `upscale` block-specific settings
- Per-block workflow caches

## Why these calls
- Gear-icon-right is the universal "meta, not destination" pattern; keeps the main nav focused on creative workflows
- `/presets` as a top-level nav promotes the marketplace as a real product surface (not a hidden utility)
- Tab 4 extras (output dir, retention) are real user-asked features that fit Settings' scope; everything else stays in-block to avoid scope creep
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] SQLite settings layer added (tables: credentials, endpoints, app_prefs). Reuse run_history.db connection layer.
- [ ] `/settings` Next.js route + gear icon entry point in the right side of NavBar.
- [ ] Tab 1 Credentials: RunPod API key, R2 (endpoint URL + access key + secret + bucket), OpenRouter, CivitAI, HuggingFace, imgbb, tmpfiles. All plaintext in DB; redacted in UI display with show/hide toggle.
- [ ] Tab 2 Endpoints: ComfyGen row (endpoint ID + network volume ID + status + GPU tier + volume size + provisioned date) and AIO trainer row. Per-row actions: Set up (launches .2 wizard), Health-check, Tear down + recreate.
- [ ] Tab 3 Storage: ComfyGen volume usage display, installed-presets table (data sourced from .3), Uninstall per-row blocked while in use, "Browse marketplace" → /presets.
- [ ] Tab 4 App: version display, output directory picker (default `./output`), run history retention dropdown (30d / 90d / 1y / Forever; default 90d), background prune job that runs daily and respects the policy, links section, license notice.
- [ ] On Validate click: RunPod key → whoami; endpoint IDs → /health; R2 creds → list buckets; HF token → whoami; imgbb/tmpfiles → ping. UI shows per-field green/red; save proceeds regardless with warning.
- [ ] Tear-down action calls RunPod API to delete the endpoint (BLOCKED on RunPod API research note); on success, clears endpoint row to unconfigured.
- [ ] Shared frontend hook `useSettings()` + backend route. All custom_blocks that today read env/localStorage migrate to Settings (cross-cut to .6 / .7 / .9).
- [ ] Old localStorage keys (`comfygen_endpoint_id`, `wan22_t2v_endpoint_id`, `wan22_i2v_endpoint_id`) are NOT read on launch — fresh start migration per D3.
- [ ] Output directory setting respected by all blocks that write local files (image_viewer, video_viewer, save-local flows, etc.).
- [ ] Run history retention prune job: daily background task; respects "Forever" by skipping; logs how many records pruned.
- [ ] `/docs/settings.md`: how to open Settings, what each credential is for + where to obtain it (RunPod, R2, OpenRouter, CivitAI, HuggingFace, imgbb, tmpfiles), endpoint actions, storage management, app preferences, troubleshooting.

## Out of scope (explicit)
- Multiple endpoints per type
- Credential encryption (plaintext; local-only)
- Cloud sync / multi-user
- Per-block preferences (stay in-block)
- Telemetry toggle (no telemetry exists yet)
- Theme override (dark-only per project rules)
notes:
RESEARCH ITEM: Verify RunPod API supports endpoint deletion (specifically: programmatic teardown via REST/GraphQL). If unsupported, the tear-down action must degrade gracefully (e.g. show 'manual deletion required, opens RunPod console').
Additional acceptance: /docs/settings.md in the BlockFlow repo covering — how to open Settings, what each credential is for + where to obtain it (RunPod, R2, OpenRouter, CivitAI), what the endpoint registry holds, how validation works, how to tear down + recreate an endpoint, troubleshooting common errors (auth failures, validation skips, etc.). Screenshots of the Settings UI included.
CROSS-CUT from .3: Settings credential schema must include HF_TOKEN (HuggingFace) in addition to RunPod / R2 / OpenRouter / CivitAI. Required when presets with hf_token_required: true are installed.

title: video-fx RIFE fails: Next proxy timeout + non-JSON error handling + subprocess stderr capture
description: Two symptoms when running Smooth on an upscaled clip: (1) frontend shows 'Unexpected token I, Internal S... is not valid JSON' — Next.js dev proxy hangs up after ~5 min undici timeout on slow /api/blocks/video_fx/run; frontend then chokes on the plain-text 500 body. (2) capture_output=True buffers all RIFE per-frame stderr in RAM for the full run. Fixes: defensive res parsing, direct localhost:8000 call for this endpoint to bypass the proxy, stderr=DEVNULL for the RIFE subprocess (capture only ffmpeg's). Fans-blowing is real Metal load — out of scope; binary is universal arm64.
close_reason: Merged locally. Proxy bypass + defensive parsing + stderr-to-file.

title: Custom LoRA + Dataset artifact cards (no pipeline detail)
description: Replace generic RunCard with dedicated DatasetCard and LoraCard when run's primary artifact is dataset or trained lora. Dataset: 2x2 thumbs + name + count + ready/needs-captioning badge (via new /api/datasets/{name}/caption-status). LoRA: image-card-sized thumbnail, trigger+model+dataset, download buttons per .safetensors, training stats block. Both: drop Restore/block-chips/Details; keep favorite+delete.

title: Upload Image: remove mode picker, dual-emit local+url via polymorphic image value
description: Today the Upload Image block forces the user to pick Local / Tmpfiles / ImgBB, but the right choice is determined entirely by downstream blocks (i2v_prompt_writer needs local bytes, dataset_create needs a fetchable URL, etc). Replace with: always save locally AND upload to tmpfiles in background, emit a polymorphic ImageRef value carrying both forms, and add shared helpers (toPublicUrls / toDisplayUrls) that every consumer block uses to pick the form it needs. Delete ImgBB endpoint.

title: HearmemanAI preset registry: new public repo + initial Wan 2.2 / LTX 2.3 / Z-Image manifests
description: New public GitHub repo (e.g. Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets). Define manifest schema: workflow JSON path, model URLs/hashes, setup steps, disk size estimate, BlockFlow version compat. Seed with three initial presets: Wan 2.2 (T2V + I2V variants), LTX 2.3, Z-Image. Each preset's workflow JSON included in the repo. Needs grilling: schema design, single repo vs per-preset repos, how versions are published, how BlockFlow fetches (raw GH vs releases vs a CDN).
design:
## Resolved scope (post-grill)

**Preset bundle:** workflow JSON + model manifest (URLs, hashes, destination paths) + optional setup script. Closest match to how ComfyGen already operates; setup script handles the long-tail (custom-node installs, model conversions, HF token usage) for presets that need more than just model download.

**Fetch mechanism:** raw GitHub content at runtime — `raw.githubusercontent.com/Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets/main/manifest.json` plus per-preset paths. No release artifacts; new presets visible the moment they merge. BlockFlow caches manifest locally (TTL ~1h).

**Versioning:** per-preset semver in manifest. BlockFlow stores installed version in Settings; UI shows "update available" when manifest > installed. No auto-update — user clicks to apply. Standard package-manager UX.

**Initial v1 set (4 presets):**
- `wan22-t2v` — Wan 2.2 text-to-video
- `wan22-i2v` — Wan 2.2 image-to-video
- `ltx-2.3` — LTX 2.3
- `z-image-turbo` — Z-Image Turbo T2I
- `qwen-image` — Qwen-Image

Each must have a tested working ComfyUI workflow that Aviv has already validated.

## Why these calls
- Setup script in bundle is the right pragmatism — most presets won't need one, but Wan 2.2 / LTX with their LightX2V distill LoRA prep or HF-gated downloads will.
- Raw GitHub fetch over Releases: fastest iteration cadence, matches the "marketplace" framing where new presets appear as they ship, not in batches.
- Per-preset semver + manual update: users get fix uptake without surprise pipeline drift.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Public GitHub repo created: github.com/Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets (MIT license).
- [ ] Repo README documents manifest schema with worked example.
- [ ] Top-level `manifest.json` at repo root listing all presets: id, version, name, description, disk size estimate, BlockFlow version compat, path to preset dir.
- [ ] Each preset has its own directory containing:
  - `preset.json` — metadata (id, version, name, description, disk size, blockflow_min_version)
  - `workflow.json` — the ComfyUI workflow ready to run
  - `models.json` — list of {url, sha256, destination_path, optional hf_token_required}
  - `setup.sh` — optional. If present, runs inside ComfyGen worker after model download
  - `README.md` — human-facing description, prompt tips, sample outputs
- [ ] Initial 5 presets seeded and end-to-end tested against a fresh ComfyGen endpoint: wan22-t2v, wan22-i2v, ltx-2.3, z-image-turbo, qwen-image.
- [ ] CI on the registry repo: validate manifest.json against schema; lint each preset.json; verify model URLs return 200.
- [ ] BlockFlow integration (consumed by .3): fetches manifest from raw.githubusercontent.com, caches with 1h TTL, falls back to cache on network failure.

## Out of scope (explicit)
- Multi-source registries (only one official registry for v1)
- User-uploaded / forked presets through BlockFlow UI
- Cryptographic signature verification (community PRs go through code review)
- Auto-update on BlockFlow launch (manual click only)
notes:
OPEN QUESTION: setup.sh execution context. Currently spec says it runs 'inside ComfyGen worker after model download' — this requires ComfyGen to expose a 'run preset script' endpoint, OR for the preset installer (.3) to package setup.sh into the model-download job. Needs cross-grill with .3 (Preset installer subsystem). If ComfyGen doesn't currently support arbitrary script execution, this might be deferred (presets without setup.sh ship at v1; setup.sh support added later).
Additional acceptance: TWO docs surfaces.
1) In BlockFlow repo /docs/presets.md — end-user docs: what presets are, how to browse the registry, how to install/uninstall, how updates work, disk-usage management.
2) In Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets repo /docs/ — preset-author docs: manifest schema reference, models.json format, setup.sh contract, contribution workflow (PR + CI), versioning conventions, testing a preset locally before PR.

title: Block codegen: scan optional private_blocks/ overlay
description: Modify the block-registry codegen (npm run predev) to also scan a private_blocks/ directory if it exists. private_blocks/ is gitignored. Public users have no such dir; Aviv keeps his private Wan 2.2 blocks there. Smallest stream, foundational for the public/private split. Needs grilling: how registration merges, name collision behavior, frontend bundle handling.
design:
## Resolved scope

Small mechanical change. Two places need to learn about `private_blocks/`:

1. **Frontend codegen** (`frontend/scripts/gen-custom-block-registry.mjs`) — currently scans only `repoRoot/custom_blocks/` for dirs containing `frontend.block.tsx`. Extend to also scan `repoRoot/private_blocks/` if it exists; merge results.

2. **Backend block-sidecar auto-loader** (`backend/main.py` — auto-loads `backend.block.py` from each block dir per project CLAUDE.md). Same overlay: scan `private_blocks/` too.

**Layout decision:** `private_blocks/` lives at repo root, sibling to `custom_blocks/`. Gitignored. Same internal structure (one dir per block with `frontend.block.tsx` + optional `backend.block.py`).

**Name collision:** if a slug exists in both dirs, ERROR at codegen time. No silent shadowing. Aviv is the only private_blocks/ consumer and won't collide intentionally; loud failure prevents subtle bugs.

## Why these calls
- Sibling dir over nested (e.g. `custom_blocks/private/`): keeps a clean separation in code reviews + gitignore.
- Error on collision over override: protects against accidental shadowing during merges; cost is near-zero (Aviv just renames).
- No new config / env / flag: directory presence is the signal. Public users have no dir → no overlay. Aviv has the dir → overlay loads.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `frontend/scripts/gen-custom-block-registry.mjs` scans both `custom_blocks/` and (if present) `private_blocks/`; concatenates the discovered slugs; generates a single `_register.ts` with imports from both dirs.
- [ ] Backend block sidecar auto-loader (`backend/main.py`) scans both dirs.
- [ ] Slug collision between the two dirs aborts codegen with a clear error message.
- [ ] `private_blocks/` added to `.gitignore`.
- [ ] `npm run predev` succeeds in three scenarios: (a) no `private_blocks/` dir at all, (b) empty `private_blocks/` dir, (c) `private_blocks/` with one or more blocks.
- [ ] Documented in `/docs/private-blocks.md`: what private_blocks/ is, when to use it, structure expectations, collision behavior. Mostly for power users who want to keep blocks out of OSS distribution (Aviv's case).

## Out of scope
- General plugin system (e.g. installable block packages)
- UI to manage private_blocks/ from inside BlockFlow
- Block discovery from arbitrary paths via env var

title: Public-build cleanup: strip Aviv defaults + move Wan 2.2 blocks to private_blocks/
description: Move generation/ and wan_22_image_to_video/ block dirs to private_blocks/ (Aviv's local only, gitignored). Remove DEFAULT_DANIELLA_LORA, LORA_SOURCE_SSH_TARGET, hearmeman-loras, hardcoded endpoint IDs from config.py + block code. Strip the lora_selector block too if it doesn't survive the rewrite. Needs grilling: what other refs slip through (Hearmeman naming in URLs, hostnames, etc), grep checklist.
design:
## Resolved scope

Final cleanup pass that makes the public BlockFlow build genuinely free of Aviv-specific state. Depends on .8 (private_blocks/ overlay must exist before blocks can be moved there).

**Move to private_blocks/ (per D10 + .7's reshape):**
- `custom_blocks/generation/` → `private_blocks/generation/` (Wan 2.2 T2V)
- `custom_blocks/wan_22_image_to_video/` → `private_blocks/wan_22_image_to_video/` (Wan 2.2 I2V)
- `custom_blocks/lora_selector/` → `private_blocks/lora_selector/` (per .7)

**Strip from public code** (grep-driven; see acceptance checklist for full token list):
- Hardcoded RunPod endpoint IDs: `17rfasn4qhfuxm`, `7cimkii50xunxw`, `x06nemnipd7rru`
- LoRA-pod SSH refs: `LORA_SOURCE_SSH_TARGET`, related env vars
- S3 bucket default: `hearmeman-loras`
- Named LoRA defaults: `DEFAULT_DANIELLA_LORA`, any other `*DANIELLA*` references
- Comment/doc references to `hearmemanai_lora_training_app_v2` (the upstream private app)
- Any `Hearmeman24/` GitHub URLs in code that point to private repos (verify post-move)

**Verification (CI gate):** add a pre-merge grep check that fails the build if any of the above tokens appear in `custom_blocks/`, `backend/`, or `frontend/src/`. Prevents accidental re-introduction.

## Why this approach
- Grep-driven cleanup with a CI check is the only durable way to prevent regression. Manual lint won't catch the next addition.
- private_blocks/ move keeps Aviv's setup functional unchanged — these blocks still work, just aren't in the public bundle.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Block dirs moved to private_blocks/: `generation/`, `wan_22_image_to_video/`, `lora_selector/`. Code unchanged inside the dirs; only the path moves.
- [ ] Verify Aviv's local setup still runs: with `private_blocks/` present, all three blocks register and function exactly as before the move.
- [ ] Grep cleanup in `custom_blocks/`, `backend/`, `frontend/src/` — zero occurrences of any of:
  - `17rfasn4qhfuxm` (Wan 2.2 T2V endpoint default)
  - `7cimkii50xunxw` (AIO trainer endpoint default)
  - `x06nemnipd7rru` (Wan 2.2 I2V endpoint default)
  - `LORA_SOURCE_SSH_TARGET` and any other `LORA_SOURCE_SSH*`
  - `hearmeman-loras` (S3 bucket)
  - `DEFAULT_DANIELLA` (any case)
  - `hearmemanai_lora_training_app_v`
- [ ] CI check: grep step in the build that fails on any of the above tokens appearing in public code paths.
- [ ] `backend/config.py` cleaned: no Aviv-named defaults; only legitimate env-var-driven values remain.
- [ ] README + /docs/ scrubbed of internal references; nothing assumes Aviv-specific infra.
- [ ] `/docs/contributing.md`: mentions the CI grep gate + the public/private block split — helps future contributors avoid landing private state in public code.

## Out of scope
- Renaming the BlockFlow repo (handled by .11)
- License changes (handled by .11)
- Any actual code logic changes (all changes here are deletions, moves, and CI additions)
notes: ADDITIONAL move target: lora_selector also moves to private_blocks/ per .7's reshape (not just generation + wan_22_image_to_video). Update .9 acceptance accordingly.

title: lora_selector: move to private_blocks/ (no rewrite needed)
description: Replace SSH-into-pod LoRA enumeration (LORA_SOURCE_SSH_TARGET) with a call to ComfyGen's existing list-loras endpoint (already used + cached by comfy_gen block). Reuse the cache layer. Strip the SSH env vars entirely. Needs grilling: cache reuse vs separate, how lora_selector handles ComfyGen-not-configured, multi-endpoint case (which ComfyGen endpoint to ask).
design:
## Resolved scope (post-grill — reshape from "rewrite" to "move private")

**Original framing rejected:** lora_selector doesn't need rewriting to use ComfyGen's list-loras endpoint.

**Actual situation:** lora_selector exists as a separate block specifically to feed LoRAs into the Wan 2.2 blocks (generation, wan_22_image_to_video). Those blocks are moving to private_blocks/ (D10). In the OSS flow, Wan 2.2 is accessed via a ComfyGen preset, and the comfy_gen block already has in-line LoRA detection (auto-detects LoraLoader / LoraLoaderModelOnly nodes from the workflow + shows dropdowns from the ComfyGen list-loras cache, per project CLAUDE.md). Public users get LoRA selection through that path; no standalone lora_selector block needed.

**Therefore:** lora_selector moves to `private_blocks/` (along with generation and wan_22_image_to_video, per .9). No rewrite. The SSH-to-pod LORA_SOURCE_SSH_TARGET model stays in the private block for Aviv's continued use.

## Why this call
- Avoids ~1 week of unnecessary refactor work on a block that has no public user
- Keeps the SSH-to-pod model functional for Aviv (his private Wan 2.2 endpoints still depend on it)
- LoRA UX in OSS is already solved by comfy_gen's existing detection — no gap

## Cross-cut
- Original D1c (LoRA discovery via ComfyGen volume) still holds for the public flow — comfy_gen block already does this via its existing list-loras endpoint call. No code changes needed there for v1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `custom_blocks/lora_selector/` directory moved to `private_blocks/lora_selector/`. Files unchanged.
- [ ] LORA_SOURCE_SSH_TARGET and related env vars referenced ONLY from `private_blocks/lora_selector/` after this move; remove any references from public code (config.py, etc.) — covered by .9.
- [ ] Verify comfy_gen block's in-block LoRA detection works end-to-end with a workflow containing LoraLoader nodes (regression check; no code change expected).
- [ ] `/docs/blocks/comfy-gen.md` (cross-cut) covers the in-block LoRA selection UX — what users see when a preset has LoRA slots, how to override defaults.

## Out of scope
- Any rewrite of lora_selector itself (stays as-is in private_blocks/)
- Public-facing standalone LoRA selector block (deferred indefinitely — comfy_gen handles this need)

title: lora_train + dataset_create blocks: R2 default + strip hardcoded fallbacks
description: Rewire both blocks to default to user-configured R2 bucket from Settings (no more hearmeman-loras default). Strip RUNPOD_LORA_ENDPOINT_ID=7cimkii50xunxw hardcoded fallback in lora_train/backend.block.py. Strip DEFAULT_DANIELLA_LORA and named LoRA defaults. Both blocks read endpoint refs from Settings registry. Needs grilling: R2 client compat (boto3 endpoint URL swap), error UX when settings missing, backwards-compat with Aviv's own env-driven setup.
design:
## Resolved scope (post-grill)

Mechanical refactor: both blocks read R2 creds + trainer/nano-banana endpoint refs from Settings (.1) via the shared `useSettings()` hook. Strip Aviv-specific defaults.

**Cleanup targets:**
- `custom_blocks/lora_train/backend.block.py`:
  - Remove `RUNPOD_LORA_ENDPOINT_ID = _env("RUNPOD_LORA_ENDPOINT_ID", default="7cimkii50xunxw")` — read from Settings only, no default
  - Remove `hearmeman-loras` S3 bucket default — read R2 bucket from Settings
  - Boto3 client construction: pass `endpoint_url` from Settings R2 endpoint URL (S3-compatible default works)
  - Remove `DEFAULT_DANIELLA_LORA` if referenced here
- `custom_blocks/dataset_create/backend.block.py`:
  - `NANO_BANANA_ENDPOINT = "google-nano-banana-2-edit"` stays (it's a public RunPod-hosted endpoint, not Aviv-specific)
  - Same boto3 endpoint URL swap for R2
  - Same hearmeman-loras removal

**Empty-state UX (general pattern, applies to both blocks):** when required Settings are missing, block renders disabled with a banner: "Configure [missing items] in Settings to use this block." Inline "Open Settings" button. No inline credential entry — keeps the named-endpoint-refs architecture clean.

## Why these calls
- Disable-with-prompt is the only UX consistent with D3 (Settings as single source). Inline credentials in blocks would recreate the duplicate-config problem.
- Nano Banana endpoint stays hardcoded because it's RunPod's public shared endpoint — not Aviv-specific. Users just need RunPod account access.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `lora_train/backend.block.py`: RUNPOD_LORA_ENDPOINT_ID hardcoded fallback removed; reads trainer endpoint ID from Settings via shared backend Settings accessor.
- [ ] `lora_train/backend.block.py`: S3 bucket / region / endpoint URL hardcoded defaults removed; boto3 client constructed with values from Settings R2 creds.
- [ ] `dataset_create/backend.block.py`: same R2 config refactor. NANO_BANANA_ENDPOINT constant retained (public RunPod endpoint).
- [ ] Frontend (both blocks): when required Settings missing, block renders disabled with informative banner naming what's missing + "Open Settings" button.
- [ ] Frontend: when Settings are present, blocks render normal UI; no inline credential entry anywhere.
- [ ] All references to `hearmeman-loras`, `DEFAULT_DANIELLA_LORA`, `RUNPOD_LORA_ENDPOINT_ID` default value (`7cimkii50xunxw`) removed from these blocks.
- [ ] `/docs/blocks/lora-train.md` and `/docs/blocks/dataset-create.md`: what each block does, what Settings are required, how to use, troubleshooting.

## Out of scope
- Wizard / Settings UI work (owned by .2 / .1)
- ComfyGen "upload trained LoRA" flow refactor (already works; no changes needed)
- Backwards-compat shim for Aviv's existing env-var-driven setup (Aviv re-enters via Settings; matches D3 fresh-start migration)

title: AIO LoRA trainer: publish public Dockerfile + source repo under MIT
description: Take aio_lora_training_serverless (/Users/avivkaplan/src/lora-training/aio_lora_training_serverless/) public: clean Aviv-specific defaults, switch S3 to R2 as default transport, add MIT LICENSE, write README for self-hosted deployment, publish image to ghcr or Docker Hub, set up the public GitHub repo. Needs grilling: which repo name, what gets stripped vs kept, image hosting decision, version tagging strategy, R2 wiring in the worker.
design:
## Resolved scope (post-grill + ComfyGen audit)

The trainer is the AIO LoRA trainer that BlockFlow's setup wizard provisions on the user's RunPod account, mirroring ComfyGen's `comfy-gen init` pattern. .5 makes the trainer publish-ready (source + image + base template). The wizard UX (which calls these artifacts) is owned by .2.

**Repo:** `Hearmeman24/blockflow-lora-trainer` (renamed from the internal `aio_lora_training_serverless`).

**Worker shape (verified by audit):** everything baked in the image, no clone-at-startup runtime-repo pattern (simpler than Wan handler). Single source repo, single image.

**Env-var contract (baked into RunPod template at creation, not per-job — matching ComfyGen's pattern):**
- `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` — R2/S3 access
- `S3_BUCKET`, `S3_REGION`, `S3_ENDPOINT_URL` — R2/S3 destination (R2 by default per D2)
- `HF_TOKEN` — optional, for HF-gated model downloads
- `MAX_TRAINING_HOURS`, `CLEANUP_DATASET_AFTER` — operational tuning with sensible defaults
- `VOLUME_ROOT` — defaults to `/runpod-volume`

**Base template strategy:** create a public `blockflow-trainer-base` RunPod template (mirrors ComfyGen's `bdy0gkebsg`). Wizard clones it via `saveTemplate` GraphQL mutation and overlays user-specific env vars at creation time. One source of truth; consistent UX with ComfyGen.

## Why these calls
- One-repo, baked-in-image shape (no runtime-repo split): simpler than the Wan handler arrangement, no additional repo to publish, no PAT plumbing.
- Env-vars-at-template-creation matches ComfyGen exactly. Zero deviation from the proven pattern; wizard code in .2 can be near-identical between the two flows. Rotation requires endpoint recreation (acceptable, rare event).
- Base template eliminates wizard work — wizard just clones + overlays, doesn't construct templates from scratch.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Public GitHub repo created: `github.com/Hearmeman24/blockflow-lora-trainer` (MIT LICENSE).
- [ ] Existing private code (`/Users/avivkaplan/src/lora-training/aio_lora_training_serverless/`) migrated; Hearmeman-internal references / hardcoded paths / TODOs scrubbed.
- [ ] README documents: env-var contract, RunPod template requirements (GPU count, volume mount), example payload schema, build-from-source instructions.
- [ ] Docker image published to ghcr.io (chosen by default — registry doesn't affect UX). Tagged with semver (e.g. `ghcr.io/hearmeman24/blockflow-lora-trainer:v1.0.0`) + `:latest`. CI auto-publish on git tag.
- [ ] Public RunPod base template created (`blockflow-trainer-base`) referencing the published image, with: volume mount `/runpod-volume`, baseline env vars (NCCL settings, `VOLUME_ROOT`), CUDA version constraints matching the image. Template ID documented in README so the wizard (.2) can reference it.
- [ ] Worker code defaults R2/S3 endpoint URL to R2 (boto3 endpoint URL config) when `S3_ENDPOINT_URL` is set; falls back to AWS when unset.
- [ ] `/docs/lora-training.md` in the BlockFlow repo: user-facing — what LoRA training is, what models can be trained, dataset format expectations, typical training durations, GPU recommendations, troubleshooting.
- [ ] `/docs/` in the trainer repo: developer-facing — architecture, env-var reference, contributing guide, how to build the image locally, how to test against a dev RunPod endpoint.

## Out of scope (explicit)
- BlockFlow UI / setup wizard work (owned by .2)
- Per-job credential plumbing (template env vars only — matches ComfyGen)
- Multi-image / multi-architecture builds (single linux/amd64 image at v1)
- Endpoint teardown semantics (owned by .1 Settings)

title: Preset installer subsystem
description: Fetch HearmemanAI preset manifest from the preset registry repo, present available presets to user with size/description/status (installed/not), on install trigger ComfyGen to download required model files, poll progress, validate, mark preset installed in Settings, expose as a selectable workflow in the comfy_gen block. Needs grilling: manifest schema, install/uninstall UX, disk-usage display, how validation works, how preset workflow JSON is shipped (inline in manifest vs separate file), versioning.
design:
## Resolved scope (post-grill)

The preset installer is BlockFlow's UI + backend logic that fetches presets from the registry (.10), drives their installation onto the user's ComfyGen endpoint, and tracks installed state in Settings (.1). It's what makes the "marketplace" experience real.

**Manifest fetch:** GET `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets/main/manifest.json`. Cache locally (1h TTL); fall back to cache on network failure. Parse the schema defined in .10.

**Install flow per preset:**
1. User clicks "Install" on a preset card → BlockFlow constructs a ComfyGen `download-models` job containing the models.json entries (url, destination_path, optional hf_token_required → pull from Settings)
2. ComfyGen worker downloads each model to its network volume (reuses ComfyGen's existing cache + download logic — the `comfy_gen` block already calls this endpoint per source)
3. BlockFlow polls progress; UI shows per-file progress bars
4. On completion, ComfyGen returns final paths + computed sha256 hashes
5. BlockFlow verifies each hash matches the manifest's expected sha256
6. On hash mismatch → preset stays "not installed" with error; user can retry. On match → mark installed in Settings (record preset id, version, install date, disk usage)
7. Optional `setup.sh`: if the preset includes one, dispatch to ComfyGen as a follow-up job (DEFERRED — see open question on .10 about setup.sh execution context)
8. Preset's workflow JSON cached locally and made selectable in `comfy_gen` block (consumed by .4)

**Uninstall flow:** confirm dialog → BlockFlow sends ComfyGen a deletion job targeting `destination_path` for each file → on success, remove from Settings → preset disappears from `comfy_gen` block dropdown → workflow JSON stays in local cache (cheap). Uninstall button greyed out while preset is in use by any running pipeline; BlockFlow's tab-scoped pipeline tracker reports which presets are active.

**Update flow:** when manifest version > installed version, preset card shows "Update available." Click → same install flow runs, but the destination paths and any orphaned files (models from the old version no longer in the new manifest) get cleaned up first.

**Disk-usage display:** Settings page surfaces a "ComfyGen volume usage" section listing each installed preset + its size + last-used date. Drives uninstall decisions.

## Why these calls
- ComfyGen-side download is the only viable path — BlockFlow doesn't have volume write access (volume is only mounted from RunPod workers). Reusing ComfyGen's existing download-models endpoint is free leverage.
- Hash check (not smoke test) is the right validation depth: catches corruption, doesn't burn 5+ minutes of compute per install for a smoke test that ComfyGen runs anyway on first job use.
- Install-blocks-uninstall protection avoids the 'why did my pipeline error halfway through' bug class with zero state-tracking complexity beyond what BlockFlow's parallel pipeline manager already has.

## Open questions / cross-cuts
- setup.sh execution context (see .10's open question): requires ComfyGen to expose script-execution capability OR setup.sh ships at v2. Decision deferred; v1 ships preset support without setup.sh; presets that need it stay un-shippable until resolved.
- hf_token plumbing: presets with hf_token_required: true must pull the user's HF_TOKEN from Settings credential store and pass it to the ComfyGen download job. Requires Settings (.1) to support HF_TOKEN as an additional credential (cross-cut — add to .1 acceptance via note).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Manifest fetch: BlockFlow GETs raw.githubusercontent.com/Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets/main/manifest.json; caches locally with 1h TTL; falls back to cache on network failure.
- [ ] Preset browser UI: page or modal listing all manifest presets with name, description, version, disk size estimate, status (installed / update available / not installed / in use), Install / Update / Uninstall buttons.
- [ ] Install flow constructs a ComfyGen download-models job from preset's models.json (url + destination_path + optional hf_token from Settings); polls progress with per-file UI.
- [ ] Sha256 verification: each downloaded file's hash compared to manifest expected. Mismatch → preset stays not-installed, error surfaced, retry available.
- [ ] Installed state persisted to Settings (.1) — preset id, version, install date, disk size. Workflow JSON cached locally and exposed for .4 (comfy_gen block dropdown).
- [ ] Uninstall: confirm dialog → ComfyGen deletion job → Settings updated → preset hidden from comfy_gen block dropdown.
- [ ] Uninstall blocked when preset is in use by any active pipeline (tab-scoped pipeline tracker check); tooltip explains why.
- [ ] Update flow: when manifest version > installed, show "Update available"; click runs install flow with cleanup of orphaned old-version files.
- [ ] Disk-usage section in Settings UI showing per-preset disk consumption + last-used; sortable.
- [ ] `/docs/presets.md`: end-user — browsing, installing, updating, uninstalling, troubleshooting (download failures, hash mismatches, disk full).
- [ ] setup.sh handling DEFERRED: v1 presets cannot include setup.sh; manifest validator (.10) rejects them; documented limitation.

## Out of scope
- Multi-source registries (only the official Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets registry)
- User-authored presets through BlockFlow UI (manifest PR is the contribution path)
- Auto-update on launch (manual click only)
- setup.sh execution (deferred to v2)
- Partial preset install (all-or-nothing per preset)

## Cross-cut to update
- .1 (Settings) acceptance: add HF_TOKEN to the credential schema. Required for any preset with hf_token_required: true.
notes: UPDATE: /presets is its own top-level Next.js page accessed via a NavBar nav item (between Generate and Artifacts, or wherever logical), NOT a modal inside Settings. The preset installer (.3) owns the /presets page UI. Settings Tab 3 'Storage' shows installed-presets summary only + a 'Browse marketplace' button that navigates to /presets.

title: Setup wizard: RunPod auth + ComfyGen/AIO trainer provisioning + R2
description: First-run wizard that takes a user from zero to working BlockFlow: collect RunPod API key, R2 creds (or guide creation), provision ComfyGen endpoint, provision AIO LoRA trainer endpoint, create/select ComfyGen network volume, write everything to Settings. Distill ComfyGen's existing endpoint-creation flow into a BlockFlow-native subsystem. Needs grilling: wizard step flow, error/retry handling, can users skip and configure manually later, what happens when wizard fails partway through.
design:
## Resolved scope (post deep-grill)

A BlockFlow setup wizard that ports `comfy-gen init`'s flow into a guided UI, supporting both fresh provisioning and attaching existing endpoints, with the trainer wired into the same flow.

**Entry:** wizard launches on first run when Settings has no endpoints. Re-launchable from Settings via per-endpoint "Set up" actions. Skip-to-Settings always available.

**Step 1 — What to set up:** checkbox selection per endpoint type: ComfyGen (default checked), LoRA trainer (default unchecked).

**Step 2 — Per-endpoint mode:** for each selected endpoint, user picks:
- **Create new** (recommended; full provisioning flow), or
- **Attach existing** (paste endpoint ID + network volume ID for ComfyGen; endpoint ID only for trainer). BlockFlow validates by calling the endpoint's `/health` (NOTE: ComfyGen needs a new `/health` endpoint built — PREREQUISITE, cross-cut to ComfyGen project) and, for ComfyGen, also calls `list-loras` to confirm reachability.

**Step 3 — Shared inputs (asked once, reused across both endpoints if both are being created):**
- RunPod API key (validated via GraphQL `gpuTypes` query)
- R2 credentials (endpoint URL + access key + secret + bucket). Round-trip upload/download test on submit. **Failure blocks proceeding** — R2 is required for lora_train and dataset_create.

**Step 4 — Per-endpoint inputs (when creating new):**
- GPU tier: ComfyGen reuses ComfyGen's existing 3-tier list (Budget / Recommended / Performance). Trainer tier list TBD (research item — needs final RunPod GPU IDs informed by Wan 2.2's 2×GPU constraint and other model variants).
- Volume size (ComfyGen default 200GB; trainer default 100GB; user-adjustable)
- Max worker count: ComfyGen default 3; trainer default 2. Both freely editable. UI shows: "RunPod accounts cap at 5 workers total by default unless manually extended by the account owner; defaults sum to 5."

**Step 5 — Provisioning (per selected create-new endpoint):** mirrors `comfy-gen init`:
- Create network volume → REST `POST /v1/networkvolumes`
- Create template → GraphQL `saveTemplate` cloning the appropriate base template (`bdy0gkebsg` for ComfyGen; `blockflow-trainer-base` from .5 for trainer); inject env vars
- Create endpoint → REST `POST /v1/endpoints` with worker max from step 4, idle 5s, 600s exec timeout, FlashBoot, QUEUE_DELAY scaler
- Resumable failure handling: progress markers persisted after each successful call; "Continue from here" retry on failure; "Start over" warns + links to RunPod console for cleanup

**Step 6 — Health polling:** poll `/v2/{id}/health` until `workers.ready > 0` or `workers.idle > 0`. UI shows stage-aware progress sourced from RunPod's `workers.{ready, idle, initializing}` counts: "queued → image downloading (first cold start ~15-20min) → worker booting → ready." Skip button at any time; Settings reflects "provisioning" until first successful job confirms ready.

**Step 7 — Persist:** write Settings (.1) — endpoint IDs, template IDs, volume IDs, GPU tier choice, max worker count, provisioned date.

## Why these calls
- Attach-existing path is critical for the day-1 user with an existing ComfyGen endpoint (Aviv on launch day); ID-only attach is the minimum viable UX.
- R2-blocks-proceed: R2 is a hard prerequisite for the trainer/dataset flows; allowing provisioning past a broken R2 leaves users with provisioned-but-unusable endpoints.
- Hard 3/2 defaults shown editable: respects the RunPod 5-worker cap as the most common case, but doesn't lie to users who have extended limits.
- Stage-aware progress UI: the 15-20min first cold-start is a real UX cliff; informed users are much more patient than uninformed ones.

## TBDs / prerequisites surfaced
- **PREREQUISITE (cross-project):** ComfyGen needs a new `/health` endpoint that BlockFlow can call for attach-existing validation. Tracked outside this epic; .2 cannot ship without it.
- **RESEARCH:** Final trainer GPU tier list (GPU IDs, datacenter mappings, multi-GPU configs). Depends on which model variants the trainer supports at v1. Should be resolved before .5 or .2 land.
- **CONFIRMED LIMITATION:** RunPod cold start ~15-20min on first deploy is inherent to the Docker image download; wizard cannot speed this up, only communicate it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Wizard component in BlockFlow UI with multi-step flow; launches on first run when no endpoints configured; re-launchable from Settings via per-endpoint "Set up" actions.
- [ ] Step 1: checkbox selection for ComfyGen + LoRA trainer.
- [ ] Step 2: per selected endpoint, choose "Create new" or "Attach existing":
  - Attach ComfyGen: input endpoint ID + network volume ID; BlockFlow validates with ComfyGen `/health` + `list-loras` calls; persists to Settings on success.
  - Attach trainer: input endpoint ID; BlockFlow validates with `/health`; persists to Settings on success.
  - Create new: continues to step 3.
- [ ] Step 3: shared inputs — RunPod API key (live GraphQL validation) + R2 creds (endpoint, access key, secret, bucket; live round-trip upload/download test). R2 failure blocks step advancement until passing test.
- [ ] Step 4: per create-new endpoint — GPU tier (ComfyGen tiers from existing ComfyGen TIERS dict; trainer tier list FINAL once research item resolved), volume size (defaults 200GB ComfyGen / 100GB trainer), max worker count (defaults 3 + 2 = 5, both editable, with helper text about RunPod's 5-worker cap).
- [ ] Step 5: provisioning per create-new endpoint via ComfyGen-pattern API calls; progress markers persisted after each step; resumable retry on failure with "Continue from here" button; "Start over" warns + opens RunPod console link.

- [ ] Step 7: Settings populated with endpoint IDs, template IDs, volume IDs, tier, max workers, provisioned date.

- [ ] `/docs/setup-wizard.md`: step-by-step walkthrough with screenshots, "create vs attach" decision guidance, troubleshooting (auth fail, R2 fail, health timeout, worker limit error), how to re-run for additional endpoints.

## Out of scope

- Endpoint teardown from inside the wizard (owned by .1 Settings)

## Cross-cuts

- Trainer GPU tier list (final IDs + multi-GPU configs) blocks step 4 acceptance — tracked as research note on this bead.

title: Add Pillow to app.py inline-script deps for lora_train
description: lora_train/backend.block.py imports PIL but app.py's PEP 723 dependencies list omits Pillow, so uv run app.py builds an env where LoRA training fails with 'No module named PIL' at job start. Add Pillow>=10 to the deps list.

title: LoRA Train Cancel button gives no feedback for up to 20s, looks broken
description: Clicking Cancel on a running LoRA train job: frontend hits /api/blocks/lora_train/cancel/{id}, backend sets cancel_requested=true and returns OK instantly. setCancelling resets to false in finally, button reverts to 'Cancel' within 100ms. Backend training thread only picks up cancel_requested at next 15s poll interval, so the live status panel keeps showing RUNNING for up to ~20s. User concludes nothing happened. Fix: keep button in 'Cancelling…' state until progress.status transitions off RUNNING; surface a short hint that cancellation is in flight.

title: Dataset Caption doesn't detect upstream Dataset Create output at edit-time
description: When a Dataset Create block is connected upstream of a Dataset Caption block, the caption block shows the 'Pick an on-disk dataset' dropdown instead of recognizing the upstream dataset. Root cause: dataset_create only calls setOutput('dataset', ...) at run completion; at edit-time the output is undefined, so downstream blocks can't bind to it. Fix: emit a placeholder dataset value from dataset_create at edit-time based on the 'Dataset name' field (mirrors what dataset_caption already does for downstream LoRA Train).

title: Open-source BlockFlow: audit + distribution decisions
description: Wisp for exploring how to make BlockFlow distributable as an open-source project for general users. Current code mixes OOTB blocks with Aviv-specific infra (private RunPod endpoints, hearmeman-loras S3 bucket, SSH-to-LoRA-pod model, named LoRA defaults). Scope: decide distribution shape, design the abstractions that unblock OSS, then promote this wisp into a permanent epic with one decision-bead per resolved choice and implementation tasks hanging off it.
design:
# BlockFlow open-source distribution — audit & open decisions

## Block audit (snapshot 2026-05-21)

### OOTB (works for any user with their own API keys)
- comfy_gen — users install ComfyGen CLI, supply their own RunPod endpoint
- civitai_share — needs user CIVITAI_API_KEY + OPENROUTER_API_KEY
- prompt_writer, i2v_prompt_writer — need user OPENROUTER_API_KEY
- upload_image_to_tmpfiles, video_loader — public tmpfiles.org
- image_upscale, upscale, video_fx, video_stitcher — local ffmpeg / upscaler
- dataset_caption, image_inspector, image_viewer, video_viewer, hitl, prompt_from_txt — frontend-only

### Configurable (works once Aviv-specific defaults are stripped)
- generation — falls back to Aviv's RUNPOD_ENDPOINT_ID env; already advanced-gated
- wan_22_image_to_video — advanced-gated, endpoint from UI/env; remove Aviv-only fallback

### Aviv-only (private infra; needs real rework)
- lora_train — hardcoded RunPod endpoint `7cimkii50xunxw` + `hearmeman-loras` S3 bucket as defaults; AIO trainer worker is Aviv's image
- dataset_create — hardcoded NANO_BANANA_ENDPOINT + `hearmeman-loras` S3 bucket
- lora_selector — SSHes into Aviv's private RunPod LoRA pod (LORA_SOURCE_SSH_TARGET) to enumerate .safetensors

## Cross-cutting concerns blocking OSS as-is
1. `hearmeman-loras` S3 bucket assumed by dataset_create + lora_train → must become user-provided bucket setting (trainer worker too)
2. Hardcoded RunPod endpoint IDs (`7cimkii50xunxw`, NANO_BANANA_ENDPOINT) baked into defaults → must move to required user config, not fallbacks
3. `LORA_SOURCE_SSH_TARGET` SSH-to-pod LoRA discovery model doesn't generalize → needs pluggable "LoRA source" abstraction (local dir / ComfyGen volume / SSH pod)
4. `DEFAULT_DANIELLA_LORA` and named defaults in config.py point at Aviv's personal library → strip
5. AIO LoRA trainer worker image is Aviv's RunPod template → publish image / Dockerfile or gate the block

## Open decisions to make in this wisp
- **D1. Distribution shape**: ship Aviv-only blocks gated behind `--advanced` with no-fallback config, OR strip them from the public build entirely?
- **D2. LoRA source abstraction**: design a pluggable interface (local dir / ComfyGen volume / SSH pod) — what's the minimum viable shape?
- **D3. AIO LoRA trainer worker**: publish the image/Dockerfile publicly, or keep lora_train as a "BYO trainer endpoint" block?
- **D4. S3 dependency**: keep S3 as the only dataset transport, or also support direct upload to ComfyGen network volume / other object stores?
- **D5. Settings UX**: where do users configure RunPod endpoint IDs / S3 creds / OpenRouter key? Per-block in UI, global settings page, .env file, or all three?
- **D6. Wan 2.5 / external RunPod endpoint blocks**: keep at all, or remove from OSS build?
- **D7. ComfyGen tight coupling**: how opinionated should BlockFlow be about ComfyGen being installed — required, optional, or pluggable?
- **D8. Repo/branding**: rename anything (hearmeman-* refs), license choice, where does the OSS repo live?

## Next steps when committing this wisp
Promote to permanent bead as an **epic** "Open-source BlockFlow"; each resolved decision above becomes a `decision`-type child bead with the chosen outcome; implementation tasks hang off the epic.
notes:
REFRAME (2026-05-21): D1 question was wrong frame. Aviv's direction: BlockFlow ships OOTB with features (not barebones) and includes an endpoint-bootstrap subsystem (distilled from ComfyGen's RunPod endpoint creation flow). Setup screen lets users provision their own RunPod endpoints for ComfyGen / AIO trainer / Wan 2.2 / Nano Banana from scratch. This collapses most 'Aviv-only' blocks into OOTB — once a user runs setup for endpoint X, block X works for them. New bottleneck shifts from 'plugin loader to hide blocks' to 'publish worker images + build endpoint-bootstrap subsystem'. Remaining truly-Aviv-only concern: lora_selector's SSH-to-LoRA-pod discovery model — needs redesign (e.g. read from ComfyGen network volume) regardless of distribution shape. D1 superseded by new decision set: D1a (worker image publishing strategy), D1b (endpoint-bootstrap UX/scope), D1c (LoRA discovery redesign).
D1a/D1b RESOLVED:
- Publish as public worker images: ComfyGen (already public), AIO LoRA trainer, Wan 2.2 (T2V+I2V served by single endpoint — audit correction)
- Nano Banana: NOT Aviv's image — it's a RunPod-provided endpoint. dataset_create just needs the user's RunPod account to have access. Audit correction: dataset_create is closer to OOTB than originally classified; only the S3 bucket dependency remains as Aviv-specific.
- Setup wizard: RunPod-only deep integration. Generic backend-agnostic blocks (Replicate/fal/etc.) tracked as separate epic (see linked bead).
- Wan 2.2 publishing is CONTINGENT on solving the LoRA-pod discovery problem (D1c) — current worker loads LoRAs from Aviv's persistent pod; must be redesigned before image can be published.
D1c RESOLVED: LoRAs live exclusively on the ComfyGen RunPod network volume. Implications:
- lora_selector backend rewrite: SSH-list → ComfyGen endpoint list call (cache already exists for this)
- lora_train post-training upload to ComfyGen volume becomes canonical (already implemented)
- Wan 2.2 worker image must be modified to mount user's ComfyGen volume (not Aviv's pod) before it can be published
- LORA_SOURCE_SSH_TARGET env var removed entirely
- DEFAULT_DANIELLA_LORA and named LoRA defaults removed; LoRA list is user-driven
- Persistent LoRA pod model dies for OSS users (Aviv can still run one privately but BlockFlow won't depend on it)
D2 RESOLVED: Keep object storage as transport, but default to Cloudflare R2 (free, S3-compatible, no AWS account needed). ComfyGen already has an R2 setup step — BlockFlow's setup wizard reuses/extends it.

Architecture:
- Dataset transport: BlockFlow (local) → R2 → AIO trainer worker downloads
- Trained LoRA transport: trainer worker → R2 → BlockFlow triggers ComfyGen download job → lands on ComfyGen network volume under loras/ (existing 'upload to ComfyGen' pattern)
- LoRA discovery for lora_selector: ComfyGen 'list loras' endpoint call (no SSH, no direct volume read)
- Wan 2.2 worker: mounts user's ComfyGen network volume directly to access LoRAs at inference time

Code impact: boto3 clients in lora_train/dataset_create work with R2 by swapping endpoint URL + creds (R2 is S3-compatible). Small change, not a rewrite.

 AWS S3 default removed entirely.
D3 RESOLVED: Global Settings page + named endpoint references.
- Settings page holds: RunPod API key, R2 creds, OpenRouter key, CivitAI key, ComfyGen endpoint(s), AIO trainer endpoint(s), Wan 2.2 endpoint(s), ComfyGen network volume ID
- Endpoint registry: user adds named endpoints ('my-comfygen', 'my-trainer'); blocks select by name from dropdown
- Setup wizard populates Settings on first run; Settings page allows ongoing edits and adding endpoints
- Persistent settings store needed (likely sqlite alongside existing run_history.db, or JSON file)
- Block refactor: existing inline endpoint ID fields become named-endpoint dropdowns + 'manual override' fallback for power users
D4 RESOLVED: Big-bang launch. Framing from Aviv: 'BlockFlow is already out for users with ComfyGen' — the OSS push is about expanding *which* features work for them, not bootstrapping a userbase. Bulk of implementation work = endpoint-bootstrap subsystem + worker image modifications, not block code itself.

D5 RESOLVED: All published worker source code goes public alongside the images — sgs-worker (AIO trainer) and the Wan 2.2 worker repo. True OSS posture; sgs-worker is already effectively readable since it's cloned at container startup.
D6 RESOLVED: ComfyGen is a hard requirement. Setup wizard always provisions ComfyGen first; everything else (LoRA storage, trained LoRA destination, network volume) depends on it. No 'no-ComfyGen' code path needed.

D7 RESOLVED: MIT license. Permissive, maximum adoption signal, standard for modern OSS.
WAN 2.2 ENDPOINT STRUCTURE VALIDATED (not single-endpoint as initially thought):

Remote repos (both private, will be made public per D5):
- AvivK5498/wan22-no-comfyui = Docker build context (corresponds to local /Users/avivkaplan/comfy/sgs-infra/)
- AvivK5498/wan22-handler = runtime handler code, cloned at container start (corresponds to local /Users/avivkaplan/comfy/sgs-worker/)

T2V vs I2V: handler.py has --task CLI arg set at container startup with choices t2v/i2v/t2i/i2i/flf2v/vace/animate/s2v/rs2v/t2av/i2av. Runtime rejects jobs whose task_type doesnt match the startup task with task_type mismatch error. Therefore: ONE endpoint = ONE task. Aviv currently runs two endpoints off the same image: 17rfasn4qhfuxm (--task t2v) and x06nemnipd7rru (--task i2v).

OSS setup wizard implication: the wizard must either (a) ask user which task(s) they want and provision one endpoint per task, (b) leave endpoint creation to the user, or (c) trigger a refactor of wan22-handler so a single endpoint dispatches multiple task types per-job. Option (c) is the cleanest UX but bigger code change; flag as candidate decision when promoting wisp.
D8 RESOLVED: True single Wan 2.2 endpoint via wan22-handler refactor (cleaner mental model worth the engineering cost).

Refactor scope (substantial — likely 1-2 weeks focused work):
1. Runner selection moves from startup (--task arg) to per-job dispatch (task_type from payload picks runner class)
2. Model loading strategy decision: lazy-load+cache vs preload-all vs swap-per-job. VRAM math drives this (14B Wan 2.2 weights differ per task). Likely lazy-load with cache, gated by available VRAM.
3. Runtime config moves from startup to per-job build
4. i2v bootstrap image pre-warm is removed or deferred
5. Remove task_type mismatch gate (handler.py lines ~999-1010)
6. Cold-start UX: first job of each task type pays model-load cost (~10-30s); subsequent jobs warm

Side benefit: Aviv personally also benefits today (switching T2V/I2V currently means two endpoints; post-refactor it's one).
MAJOR REFRAME #2 (2026-05-21): Strategic pivot driven by RunPod 5-worker total limit. Cant ask users to deploy 3 endpoints.

NEW v1 ARCHITECTURE:
- 2 user-deployed endpoints only: ComfyGen + AIO LoRA trainer
- ComfyGen becomes the SINGLE generation channel for all models (Wan 2.2, LTX 2.3, Z-Image, etc.)
- Users get freeform ComfyGen workflow input PLUS one-click HearmemanAI workflow presets
- Each preset = workflow JSON + model manifest + setup steps; BlockFlow installs the preset onto users ComfyGen endpoint, validates, unlocks it as a default option
- Future: HearmemanAI endpoint/workflow marketplace inside BlockFlow (community/Aviv-branded preset packs)

DECISION REVISIONS:
- D1a REVISED: Publish list shrinks. Out v1: wan22-handler + wan22-no-comfyui (standalone Wan 2.2 endpoint deferred to post-v1, low priority). In v1: only aio_lora_training_serverless (ComfyGen already public).
- D8 CANCELLED: wan22-handler per-job task dispatch refactor is no longer a v1 dependency. Reconsidered if/when standalone Wan 2.2 endpoint becomes a priority.

NEW DELIVERABLE (D9): HearmemanAI preset registry — separate repo/artifact from BlockFlow. Holds curated workflow JSONs + model manifests + install steps. Updates independently of BlockFlow releases.

CASUALTIES IN EXISTING CODE:
- generation block (Wan 2.2 T2V, endpoint 17rfasn4qhfuxm) — deprecated for OSS or refactored into a preset launcher that delegates to comfy_gen
- wan_22_image_to_video block (endpoint x06nemnipd7rru) — same fate

ENDPOINT MATH FOR v1:
- User-deployed: ComfyGen (1) + AIO trainer (1) = 2 slots, leaves 3 for parallelism/ad-hoc
- RunPod-shared (no slot used): Nano Banana for dataset_create
- Total worker budget headroom maintained

NEW v1 CONCERN: ComfyGen network volume sizing. Presets accumulate (~28GB Wan 2.2 + ~10GB LTX 2.3 + Z-Image + VAEs/text encoders). Settings needs an installed-presets manager with disk usage + uninstall.
D9 RESOLVED: HearmemanAI preset registry lives in a separate public GitHub repo (e.g. Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets). BlockFlow fetches the manifest at runtime. Independent release cadence. Community PRs welcome. Foundation for the future marketplace.

D10 RESOLVED: generation + wan_22_image_to_video blocks removed from public OSS build. Aviv keeps them in private use (still pointing at private standalone Wan 2.2 endpoints). Public users get Wan 2.2 via the ComfyGen preset.

IMPLICATION D11 (auto-resolved by D10): 'Remove from public build, keep private' means BlockFlow's block-registry codegen must scan an additional optional directory beyond custom_blocks/ — e.g. private_blocks/ (gitignored). Public users have no private_blocks/ → only public blocks load. Aviv keeps his Wan 2.2 blocks in private_blocks/ → still functional. This is a MINIMAL plugin/overlay loader — compatible with the turnkey direction (D1), not contradicting it; the loader is just for Aviv's private extras, not for general extensibility. Implementation cost: a few lines in npm run predev codegen.
GLOBAL ACCEPTANCE RULE (applies to every child bead): Each feature must ship with user-facing documentation in a /docs folder of the BlockFlow repo. One MD file per feature/concept (e.g. /docs/settings.md, /docs/setup-wizard.md, /docs/presets.md, /docs/blocks/<block-name>.md). Docs are part of the feature's done definition; no bead closes without docs merged. The HearmemanAI preset registry repo also has its own /docs for preset authors.

title: Upload Image block fails on large files with cryptic JSON parse error
description: The Upload Image block's tmpfiles/imgbb upload posts raw image bytes through the Next.js dev proxy. Next's rewrite layer (or upstream) returns plain-text 'Internal Server Error' for large bodies, which the frontend tries to JSON.parse, surfacing a SyntaxError. Fix: client-side resize/re-encode before upload (preserve format where reasonable, cap max edge), and make the frontend gracefully report non-JSON responses.
close_reason: Fixed via client-side resize + defensive JSON parse

title: Dataset Creation block (Nano Banana 2 / RunPod) + new 'dataset' data type
description:
# Dataset Creation Block (Nano Banana 2 / RunPod) + `dataset` data type

## Goal

Port the reference project's dataset-generation flow (`hearmemanai_lora_training_app_v2`, Generate tab) into sgs-ui as a first-class pipeline block, **scoped to the RunPod Nano Banana 2 Edit endpoint only**. Along the way, introduce a new pipeline data type `dataset` that flows through the block graph and is renderable by existing viewers and the artifacts tab.

Reference parity points (from `fal_generator.py:398-612` and `dataset.py:1455-1510` in the reference repo):
- Endpoint: `POST https://api.runpod.ai/v2/google-nano-banana-2-edit/run`
- Payload: `{ prompt, images: [refUrl, ...], resolution: "1k"|"2k"|"4k", aspect_ratio, enable_safety_checker: false }`
- Polling: `/status/{job_id}`, 2s→10s backoff, 900s timeout
- Concurrency: `asyncio.Semaphore(10)` over per-image workers; cancellable

## Scope

### In
- New block `custom_blocks/dataset_create/` (Nano Banana 2 only)
- New reusable block `custom_blocks/multi_image_loader/` (up to 14 images, emits `image[]`)
- New port kind `PORT_DATASET = 'dataset'`
- Extension of `image_viewer` to accept `dataset` input
- Artifacts tab: new `dataset` media-kind filter and card rendering
- New prompt-pack store under `sgs-ui/prompt_packs/` with a backend index API
- Backend orchestrator that fans out N RunPod jobs under a semaphore and persists the resulting dataset to disk

### Out (deferred)
- Other providers (Seedream, FAL, OpenAI, Nxdify) — RunPod-only this round
- Character / collaborator management (reference project concept)
- Prompt cleaning, AI prompt generator
- Dataset editing UI (delete/replace images post-generation) — viewer is read-only v1

---

## Decisions

### D1 — Multi-image input: separate reusable block
Create `multi_image_loader` (max 14 files) emitting `PORT_IMAGE` as an array. The dataset block declares one `images` input of `PORT_IMAGE`. **Why:** keeps single-purpose ethos (comfy_gen, image_viewer already accept arrays). Future blocks needing multi-ref input (e.g., multi-ref comfy_gen) reuse it without changes.

### D2 — New port kind: `PORT_DATASET = 'dataset'`
Add to `frontend/src/lib/pipeline/registry.ts`. The runtime port system is `string & {}` (registry.ts:6) so no type registry change required — only declarations on producing/consuming blocks. Shape of a dataset value:
```ts
type DatasetValue = {
  kind: 'dataset'
  id: string                 // run id
  name: string               // user-supplied label
  images: string[]           // ordered URLs (local /outputs/datasets/<id>/img_NNN.png)
  manifest: {
    provider: 'nano-banana-runpod'
    quality: '1k' | '2k' | '4k'
    aspect_ratios: string[]   // ratios used
    prompt_pack_ids: string[]
    prompts: Array<{ index: number; prompt: string; aspect_ratio: string }>
    reference_image_urls: string[]
    created_at: string
    count: number
  }
}
```

### D3 — Image viewer accepts both `image` and `dataset`
`image_viewer` adds a second input `dataset` of kind `PORT_DATASET`. When `dataset` is present it takes precedence and renders:
- **Header strip**: dataset name, image count, provider, created_at, "Open in Artifacts" link
- **Grid**: same image grid as today, sourced from `dataset.images`
- **Prompts panel** (collapsible, default closed): list of `{index, aspect_ratio, prompt}` with copy-prompt button per row
- **Lightbox**: existing behavior + prompt overlay on hover

A `dataset` input does **not** forward as `image[]` automatically — viewer-only. Downstream blocks wanting raw images can consume the new `images` output of the dataset block.

### D4 — Dataset block outputs: `dataset` AND `image[]`
```ts
outputs: [
  { name: 'dataset', kind: PORT_DATASET },
  { name: 'images',  kind: PORT_IMAGE }, // ordered, same as dataset.images
]
```
This means a user can wire the dataset block into `image_viewer` either way, and into pipelines that operate per-image (e.g., upscaler) without unpacking.

### D5 — Artifacts tab: new `dataset` filter, collage card
- Extend `MediaKindFilter` in `frontend/src/lib/api.ts:23` with `'dataset'`.
- Add filter button in `components/run-history.tsx:13-18`.
- Backend `routes.py /api/runs` accepts `media_kind=dataset` and classifies a run as dataset when its primary artifact is `manifest.json` under `outputs/datasets/`.
- New card variant: 2×2 collage of first 4 images + dataset name + image count badge + provider tag. Click expands to full grid (modal) with prompts panel; "Send to Image Viewer" action wires it into a new pipeline tab.

### D6 — Prompt pack storage (improved over reference)
Reference uses scattered top-level JSONs (`expressions.json`, `poses.json`, `clothing.json`) + `prompt_sets.json` indexing text files. We replace with **one JSON file per pack** under `sgs-ui/prompt_packs/<pack_id>.json`:
```json
{
  "id": "lifestyle_v1",
  "title": "Lifestyle (everyday scenes)",
  "description": "Mirror selfies, cafés, kitchens, etc.",
  "category": "scene",
  "mode": "random",
  "tags": ["lifestyle", "everyday"],
  "prompts": [
    "Mirror selfie holding an iPhone in a bright bathroom",
    "Sitting on a kitchen counter at golden hour"
  ],
  "created_at": "2026-05-20T..."
}
```
Backend exposes `GET /api/blocks/dataset_create/prompt_packs` returning a manifest. **Why one-file-per-pack:** trivially shareable (drop a file in a folder), git-friendly diffs, no central file to lock-contend. Improvement over reference: explicit `category` + `mode` allows future combinatorial sampling (`combinatorial` mode pulls one prompt from each of N selected packs and composes them — out of scope for v1, but the schema reserves the slot). The reference's `clothing.json` (a dict keyed by image path) is anti-pattern; we drop it.

**v1 sampling:** user multi-selects packs → backend pools all prompts → samples N with replacement, randomized per image. Each sampled prompt is logged in `manifest.prompts`.

### D7 — RunPod API key handling
- Backend `config.py` already exposes `RUNPOD_API_KEY` from `.env` (confirmed at `backend/config.py:33`).
- New endpoint `GET /api/blocks/dataset_create/health` returns `{ ok, runpod_key_present, prompt_pack_count }`.
- Frontend behavior:
  - If `runpod_key_present` → show read-only "✓ Loaded from .env" badge + collapsed "Override" toggle that swaps in a password field (stored in `sessionStorage` only, never persisted to disk).
  - If missing → password field required, with inline help link to where to set `RUNPOD_API_KEY`. Block refuses to execute without a key.

### D8 — Concurrency: backend orchestrator, frontend polls
Reference runs the semaphore in-process. sgs-ui's pipeline runs blocks frontend-side, but firing 50+ RunPod calls from the browser is fragile (tab close kills them, no shared rate limiting). Decision: **backend owns orchestration**.

- `POST /api/blocks/dataset_create/run` → returns `{ job_id }`, kicks off an asyncio task wrapping `asyncio.Semaphore(10)` over per-image workers (mirror reference dataset.py:1455-1510).
- `GET /api/blocks/dataset_create/status/{job_id}` → `{ status, total, completed, failed, partial_images: [...], dataset?: DatasetValue }`.
- Frontend block polls every 2s, surfaces progress in the block UI (`X / N images`), and on completion calls `setOutput('dataset', ...)` and `setOutput('images', [...])`.
- `signal.aborted` from the pipeline runner → `DELETE /api/blocks/dataset_create/jobs/{job_id}` which sets a `cancel_requested` flag the workers check (same pattern as reference dataset.py).
- Failures: partial datasets are kept. Manifest records `failed_indices`. Block surfaces a warning if `failed > 0` but still emits the dataset.

### D9 — Reference images: upload before submit
RunPod needs publicly-reachable URLs in the `images` array. The local `/outputs/...` server isn't reachable from RunPod. Reuse the `upload_image_to_tmpfiles` flow inside the backend orchestrator — upload each reference once, cache the resulting URL by file fingerprint for the duration of the job. Up to 14 references, all sent as the `images` array per RunPod request.

### D10 — Dataset persistence on disk
Mirror reference layout under `sgs-ui/outputs/datasets/`:
```
outputs/datasets/<slug(name)>_<id>/
  manifest.json         # the DatasetValue.manifest
  prompts.json          # full per-image prompts (also embedded in manifest)
  img_001.png ... img_NNN.png
  .references/
    ref_01.png ... ref_14.png
```
`slug(name)` is `slugify(name)` to make the folder human-readable while `id` keeps it unique.

### D11 — Block sizing & layout
`huge` (540x580, amber) — needs room for reference grid (up to 14 thumbs), prompt-pack multi-select, params, and progress.

---

## Block UI sketch

```
┌─ Dataset Create (Nano Banana 2) ─────────────────────────────┐
│ Dataset name   [ ____________________ ]                       │
│ Quality        ( 1k ) ( 2k ) ( 4k )                           │
│ Aspect ratios  [☑ 1:1] [☑ 9:16] [ ] 16:9 [ ] 4:3 [ ] 3:4     │
│ Image count    [────●──────] 24                               │
│                                                               │
│ Prompt packs   [ Lifestyle v1 ✕ ] [ Studio v2 ✕ ] [+ add ▾ ]  │
│                Pool size: 142 prompts · Preview              │
│                                                               │
│ Reference images (input: images, 0/14)                        │
│ ┌──┐ ┌──┐ ┌──┐  drag from multi_image_loader upstream         │
│ └──┘ └──┘ └──┘                                                │
│                                                               │
│ RunPod API key ✓ Loaded from .env       [Override ▾]          │
│                                                               │
│ Status: idle                                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

While running: status line becomes `Generating 7 / 24 · 1 failed` with a thin progress bar; thumbnails stream in as they complete.

---

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `multi_image_loader` block exists, accepts up to 14 files, emits `PORT_IMAGE` as ordered array.
- [ ] `dataset_create` block exists, RunPod-only, with all fields above.
- [ ] `PORT_DATASET` registered; `image_viewer` accepts dataset input and renders header + grid + collapsible prompts panel.
- [ ] Concurrency caps at 10 in-flight RunPod jobs; pipeline cancel kills in-flight workers within ~2s.
- [ ] Partial completion writes a valid dataset with `failed_indices` recorded.
- [ ] `RUNPOD_API_KEY` in `.env` is auto-detected; UI surfaces "Loaded from .env" badge.
- [ ] Prompt packs load from `sgs-ui/prompt_packs/*.json`; at least 2 seed packs ship in the repo (`lifestyle_v1.json`, `studio_v1.json`).
- [ ] Artifacts tab has a `Dataset` filter, renders collage cards, "Send to Image Viewer" works.
- [ ] `npm run build` passes.
- [ ] Manual run end-to-end produces a folder under `outputs/datasets/` with manifest + N images + uploaded references cached.

## Open questions (resolved here, but worth revisiting)
- **Combinatorial prompt composition** — schema reserves `mode` field; v1 ships `random` only.
- **Per-image aspect-ratio assignment** — round-robin over the multi-selected ratios (deterministic given seed), not random. Reproducible.
- **Dataset re-export / "send to LoRA training"** — out of scope; will be a future block that consumes `PORT_DATASET`.

## File map (planned)
- `frontend/src/lib/pipeline/registry.ts` — add `PORT_DATASET`
- `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` — add `'dataset'` to `MediaKindFilter`
- `frontend/src/components/run-history.tsx` — dataset filter button
- `frontend/src/components/run-card.tsx` — collage variant for datasets
- `custom_blocks/multi_image_loader/` (new) — frontend.block.tsx
- `custom_blocks/dataset_create/` (new) — frontend.block.tsx + backend.block.py
- `custom_blocks/image_viewer/frontend.block.tsx` — add dataset input + dataset-mode render
- `backend/routes.py` — extend `media_kind` accepted values
- `backend/config.py` — (already has `RUNPOD_API_KEY`; no change)
- `prompt_packs/lifestyle_v1.json`, `prompt_packs/studio_v1.json` — seed packs
acceptance_criteria: multi_image_loader + dataset_create blocks shipped; PORT_DATASET registered and rendered by image_viewer; backend semaphore caps RunPod fan-out at 10 with cancellable workers; RUNPOD_API_KEY auto-detected from .env; prompt packs load from sgs-ui/prompt_packs/*.json with 2 seed packs; Artifacts tab has Dataset filter + collage card; npm run build passes; manual e2e produces outputs/datasets/<slug>_<id>/ with manifest+images
notes:
Implementation landed. Stress-tested live against RunPod `google-nano-banana-2-edit` with the user's key.

## What shipped vs the plan

| Decision | Status |
|---|---|
| D1 multi_image_loader as a separate block | **DROPPED** — `upload_image_to_tmpfiles` already supported multi-file array output (`image[]`). Reused it. Less code, same UX. |
| D2 PORT_DATASET | shipped in `frontend/src/lib/pipeline/registry.ts` |
| D3 image_viewer dataset input | shipped — header strip + Show/Hide prompts toggle + per-image prompt panel with copy button |
| D4 dataset block outputs both `dataset` + `images` | shipped |
| D5 Artifacts dataset filter + collage card | shipped — `MediaKindFilter` extended, `_primary_media_kind` priorities `dataset` first, RunCard gets a 2x2 collage variant |
| D6 prompt packs as one JSON per file | shipped under `prompt_packs/`, seeded with `ugc_v1` (24 prompts) + `realistic_v1` (48 prompts) extracted from reference project |
| D7 RUNPOD_API_KEY detection + override | shipped via `/health` endpoint + "Loaded from .env" badge + Override toggle (sessionStorage only, never persisted) |
| D8 backend orchestrator, semaphore=10, cancellable | shipped in `custom_blocks/dataset_create/backend.block.py`. Verified live: 8 concurrent jobs all submitted in <5s, mid-flight `/cancel/{job_id}` propagated within one poll cycle (5s) and produced a PARTIAL dataset preserving the 7 completed images. |
| D9 references uploaded once | per-job — references passed in as URLs from upstream block |
| D10 dataset persistence layout | shipped — `output/datasets/<slug>_<id8>/{img_NNN.png, manifest.json}` |
| D11 huge block size | shipped |

## Bonus: ImgBB upload mode added to `upload_image_to_tmpfiles`
tmpfiles.org was returning 422 / hanging during testing. ImgBB is more reliable for RunPod's safety-filter inspection. Added:
- `IMGBB_API_KEY` env var read in `backend/config.py`
- `POST /api/blocks/upload_image_to_tmpfiles/upload-imgbb` (base64 + key form-encoded)
- `GET /api/blocks/upload_image_to_tmpfiles/health` → `{imgbb_key_present}`
- Frontend toggle: Local / Tmpfiles / **ImgBB** (3-way)
- `.env.example` documents IMGBB_API_KEY

## Real-world findings during stress testing

1. **RunPod response shape** — `google-nano-banana-2-edit` puts the result URL at `output.result` (not `images` / `image_url`). Fixed in `_extract_image_url` + added top-level `result` fallback.
2. **Aspect ratio enforcement verified** — sent `["1:1","16:9","9:16"]` round-robin across 8 images, PNGs came back at 1024x1024 / 1376x768 / 768x1376 exactly as expected.
3. **Cost** — each Nano Banana 2 generation reports `cost: 0.0875` in the RunPod response. An 8-image dataset is ~$0.70.
4. **Safety filter** — RunPod's content safety filter rejects both references and prompts. The block records the rejection in `manifest.prompts[i].error` and surfaces it as a PARTIAL dataset rather than failing the whole job.
5. **Timing** — single job warm ~30s; cold-start can add 60-90s. 8-image batch of mixed warm/cold completed in 155s end-to-end at concurrency=10.

## Test artifacts (in worktree, not committed)
- `output/datasets/stress_8_full_a8277360/` — clean 8/8 COMPLETED with 3 aspect ratios
- `output/datasets/integrated_test_9ef7ba5a/` — 2/2 via the new ImgBB upload path
- `output/datasets/stress_8_effede90/` — 7/8 PARTIAL via mid-flight cancellation
- `output/datasets/live_test_3_ac0074d5/` — 0/3 FAILED (RunPod safety filter on vault refs)

- `frontend/src/components/run-history.tsx` (+ Dataset filter button)
