- `custom_blocks/dataset_create/` (new — backend + frontend)
- `custom_blocks/image_viewer/frontend.block.tsx` (dataset input + render mode)
- `custom_blocks/upload_image_to_tmpfiles/{frontend,backend}.block.py` (+ ImgBB mode)
- `prompt_packs/{ugc_v1,realistic_v1}.json` (new — seed packs)
- `.env.example` (+ IMGBB_API_KEY doc)

title: video_fx: LUT path → file picker
description: Replace the free-text LUT path input in the video_fx block with a file picker. Upload selected .cube file to backend, cache by content hash, use cached path for ffmpeg.

title: Artifacts: show video output from every block in run details
description: In the run card's expanded details, only Video Viewer blocks show their video output. Video Stitcher, Upscale, Video FX (and other media-emitting blocks) only render their label — their video is suppressed by a stale 'skip image/video outputs that duplicate the primary artifact' branch. Fix: render any non-primary block's video/image as a collapsible details panel (same UI as viewer blocks). Primary block's media remains shown only at the top of the card.

title: Director Mode: remove length stepper UI, JSON-only length
description: Per-row length stepper (em-dash placeholder + ▲▼ buttons) removed entirely. Length is controlled only by JSON. When a prompt has a length, render a small read-only 'Ns' badge; when null, render nothing.

title: Video FX: 'Match source' fps toggle for Smooth (default on)
description: When Smooth is on, default output fps to the probed source fps instead of forcing 60. Adds a 'Match source' checkbox; when on, hides the manual Target fps input and the backend probes src fps for both RIFE multiplier selection and output -r.
close_reason: Merged to main. Match-source default-on; end-to-end verified across 2x, 0.5x match, and 60fps non-match cases.

title: Director Mode: collapse rows, remove reorder arrows, center controls
description: UX refinements to director-mode prompt rows in both blocks: (1) remove move-up/down arrows (no in-place reordering); keep only X remove + 🎚 LoRA + length stepper. (2) Center the right-side controls vertically against the textarea. (3) Prompt rows collapsed by default: show only the description text (or 'Prompt N — No description' fallback). Click description to expand the textarea. Per-row session state for expanded/collapsed; defaults to collapsed.

title: Video FX: optical flow (minterpolate) for smooth speed change
description: Speed-up/slow-down on video-fx block currently uses naive setpts, producing judder/duplicated frames. Add a 'Smooth' toggle that pipes the output through ffmpeg's minterpolate (mci/aobmc/bidir) so motion is reconstructed via optical flow.
close_reason: Merged to main (528bd9b). RIFE optical flow swap end-to-end verified.

title: Director Mode: per-prompt LoRA override (popover)
description: Each director-mode prompt can carry its own LoRA set that overrides the block-level LoRAs for that submission. UI: per-row 🎚 N LoRAs button opens a popover with the existing LoRA selector (reuses {name, branch: high|low|both, strength}). JSON: optional per-prompt loras array. Skill emits when explicitly told. Affects: generation + wan_22_image_to_video blocks, /cinematic-prompts skill, JSON parser. Depends on sgs-ui-tnm landing first.
design:
Decisions from grilling 2026-05-19 (updated):

LORA SCOPE:
1. Merge semantics: per-prompt loras are ADDITIVE to block-level loras at submit. Block-level always applies.
2. Popover UI: mirror lora_selector block. Two sections (High Noise, Low Noise), each with Add + per-row LoraRow. Source: /api/blocks/lora_selector/loras.
3. Skill emission: NEVER. LoRAs are user-only via UI. Parser still accepts `loras: LoraEntry[]` per prompt for hand-written JSONs.
4. Button label: "🎚 N LoRAs" / "🎚 + LoRA".
5. State: directorPromptLoras: LoraEntry[][] parallel to directorPrompts. Synced on add/move/remove. Reset on Director Mode off and JSON load.
6. Submit: effectiveLoras = [...blockLevelLoras, ...directorPromptLoras[idx]] filtered to name !== '__none__'.

UI FIXES (added by user request 2026-05-19):
7. Description rendering: render as PLAIN TEXT (not Input) on the same row as the prompt number, sitting above the textarea. Nothing rendered when description is empty. Set only via JSON load — no inline edit UI. Drop the Input element entirely.
8. Replace-with selector: HIDE when Director Mode is on. The upstream prompt-source selector at the top of the Prompt section is irrelevant when prompts come from the director list.

SKILL UPDATE (added by user request 2026-05-19):
9. Add a new "Wan 2.2 prompt framework" section to cinematic-prompts SKILL.md (keep existing content). Cover: 80-120 word target; shot order (opening → camera motion → reveal); camera verb vocab (pan, tilt, dolly, orbital arc, crane); motion modifiers (slow-motion, whip-pan, time-lapse; parallax cues); aesthetic tags (lighting, color-grade, lens/style); temporal params (≤5s, ≤120 frames, 16 fps test / 24 fps publish); default negative prompt verbatim; observation that 2.2 reliably respects camera verbs and roll/tilt/dolly-out unlike 2.1.

Files: director-prompts-json.{ts,test.ts}, director-load-json-button.tsx, NEW director-prompt-loras-popover.tsx, generation/frontend.block.tsx, wan_22_image_to_video/frontend.block.tsx, ~/.claude/skills/cinematic-prompts/SKILL.md (only Wan 2.2 section addition).

title: Director Mode: per-prompt LoRA override (popover)
description: Each director-mode prompt can carry its own LoRA set that overrides the block-level LoRAs for that submission. UI: per-row "🎚 N LoRAs" button opens a popover with the existing LoRA selector. JSON: optional per-prompt  ({name, branch: 'high'|'low'|'both', strength}). Skill emits when explicitly told. Affects: generation + wan_22_image_to_video blocks, /cinematic-prompts skill, JSON parser. Depends on sgs-ui-tnm landing first (shares the per-prompt object structure).

title: Director Mode: per-prompt length + header UX
description: Three threads merged into one bead: (1) Extend JSON convention with optional per-prompt length in seconds (2-5s @ 16 FPS, snapped to 4n+1). When JSON has lengths, block uses per-prompt frames and the global Frames input is off by default with override toggle. (2) Per-prompt short description (≤50 chars) shown above the prompt textarea. Optional in JSON. Skill emits when a caption is meaningful. (3) Redesign the Director Mode header row: stacked rows pattern (Director Mode + Switch / Load JSON / Loaded: <name>) instead of single crammed line. Per-prompt LoRA override deferred to a separate bead.
design:
Decisions from grilling 2026-05-19:

1. JSON: extend prompts to accept heterogeneous string | {text, length?}. Length = integer seconds 2-5, clamped. Strings still accepted (back-compat). Frames = length*16+1 (always 4n+1).

2. State: directorPromptLengths: (number|null)[] parallel to directorPrompts. useBlockFramesOverride: bool.

3. Frames-input rule:
   - ALL have length + override OFF -> Frames dimmed, ignored, per-prompt frames used.
   - SOME or NONE have length -> Frames enabled; length-less prompts use block Frames.
   - Override toggle "Use block frames" only shown when ALL have length.

4. Per-prompt UI: small stepper (min 2, max 5) per row, suffix "s", tooltip "<N>f". Empty = "—" (falls back to block Frames).

5. Header redesign (Two stacked rows):
   Row 1 (existing): "▼ Prompt … Replace with [Manual▾]"
   Row 2: "Director Mode (N prompts)" + Switch
   Row 3 (when on): "[ Load JSON ]"
   Row 4 (when loaded): "Loaded: <name>"
   Then prompts list.

6. Skill update: when controlling narration pacing, emit {text, length} objects; otherwise emit strings. Document the 16fps / 4n+1 / 2-5s range mapping.

Files: director-prompts-json.{ts,test.ts}, director-load-json-button.tsx, generation/frontend.block.tsx, wan_22_image_to_video/frontend.block.tsx, ~/.claude/skills/cinematic-prompts/SKILL.md.

Verification: must inspect each block's submit loop to confirm per-prompt frames override is wireable before touching submit code.

title: Director Mode: Load JSON prompts + skill output convention
description: In blocks that have Director Mode (generation, wan_22_image_to_video), add a Load JSON button (visible only when Director Mode is on) that loads a prompts JSON following a defined convention. Update /cinematic-prompts skill to emit JSON in that convention. Display the JSON's name above the prompt list.
design:
Decisions from grilling 2026-05-19:

1. Scope: Both Director-Mode blocks get parity — generation and wan_22_image_to_video.
2. JSON convention (minimal): { name: string, prompts: string[] }. Name optional in input (block falls back to filename stem); always written by skill.
3. Load behavior: replace existing prompts (no append, no confirm). Matches "load a JSON" mental model.
4. File picker: native <input type="file" accept=".json"> — client-side parse. No backend round-trip.
5. Name display: small muted "Loaded: <name>" above prompt list; persists across edits, cleared only by next load or Director Mode off.
6. Skill output: write JSON to ./director_prompts/<slug>.json (kebab-slug from name, timestamp suffix on collision) AND keep inline prompt rendering. Print final path.
7. Skill `name` field: human-readable title derived from user's request (e.g. "Tarantino diner sequence"). Filename is slug of same.
8. Validation on load: must be object with prompts:string[]. Invalid → inline red error under button; current prompts untouched. Lenient mode rejected.

State added per block: loadedJsonName session state. Parse/validate extracted to a pure helper for unit testing.

Out of scope (deferred): backend-stored library, paste-JSON dialog, per-prompt metadata, drag-drop.

title: ComfyGen: per-chain Add LoRA with chain detection
description: Allow users to add additional LoRA loaders to a workflow loaded in the ComfyGen block without manually editing the workflow JSON. Detect LoRA chains in the parsed workflow, render each chain under a colored badge, and give each chain a '+ Add LoRA' button that splices a duplicate loader into that chain at submit time.
design:
Decisions from grilling session 2026-05-17:

1. Persistence: Runtime-only. Added LoRAs live in block session state ('addedLoras' keyed by chain_id), spliced into the workflow JSON only at submit time. Original parsed workflow stays untouched (mirrors how bypass_loras works today). Workflow file on disk is never mutated.

2. New LoRA defaults: Match the chain's variant (LoraLoader if chain has CLIP wiring, LoraLoaderModelOnly otherwise). Pre-fill lora_name + strengths from the last loader in the chain so 'add' is literally a duplicate the user then renames.

3. Removability: Added LoRAs get an X button to remove. Visually marked (dashed left border + 'added' tag) so the user can tell them from originals. Original loaders keep only the existing bypass toggle.

4. Empty case: If no LoRA loaders are detected in the workflow, hide the add UI entirely. We don't try to invent a chain from scratch — too unreliable in complex workflows.

5. Chain detection: Extend backend _detect_lora_nodes to compute connected components via _model_source (which it already tracks) and assign each loader a chain_id. Frontend groups by chain_id and assigns a stable color from a sequential palette (emerald/violet/amber/rose).

6. Splice mechanics: New backend helper _insert_lora_node is the inverse of the existing _bypass_lora_nodes. Anchor = last loader in chain. Find every node whose inputs reference [anchor_id, 0] (MODEL) and [anchor_id, 1] (CLIP, LoraLoader only); rewrite those refs to [new_id, 0/1]; wire new loader's model/clip inputs from anchor's outputs. Allocate new node_id as max(existing) + 1.

7. Submit path: Apply added-LoRA splices in the workflow build step in backend.block.py, after bypass_loras processing, for both single-run and matrix (axis) paths.

Reliability scope: Reliable for standard LoraLoader / LoraLoaderModelOnly in linear chains. Custom variants (rgthree Power Lora, stacker nodes) and Reroute nodes between loaders are out of scope — those workflows won't show as detected chains, so add UI won't appear for them.
acceptance_criteria:
- Workflow with 1 LoraLoader chain: chain badge renders; '+ Add LoRA' button appends a row pre-filled from the existing loader; submitting the job sends a workflow with 2 chained LoraLoader nodes wired correctly.
- Workflow with 2 separate chains: 2 distinct colored badges; each chain's '+ Add LoRA' splices only into its own chain; cross-chain isolation verified by inspecting submitted workflow.
- LoraLoaderModelOnly chain: added loader is also LoraLoaderModelOnly, no CLIP wiring; submits successfully.
- Removing an added LoRA returns the submitted workflow to its pre-add state for that chain.
- Workflow with zero LoRA loaders: no add UI is rendered.
- npm run build passes.
notes: Implementation complete on branch feat/comfygen-add-lora in .worktrees/feat-comfygen-add-lora. Backend: chain_id in _detect_lora_nodes, new _insert_lora_nodes, /run accepts added_loras. Frontend: chain-grouped UI with colored badges, per-chain Add LoRA button, removable added rows (dashed border + X). Tests: 7 new backend tests (all pass), 36 existing frontend tests still pass, npm run build clean. NOT manually UI-tested — needs user verification in browser before close. Commit 4559676, not pushed.

title: Add media-kind and prompt-contains filters to artifacts page
description: Add two server-side filters to /artifacts: (1) media kind = video/image/other derived from primary artifact, (2) prompt-contains substring search over metadata.prompt only. URL-state per project convention. Python-side filter on parsed block_results JSON.
design:
Decisions:
- Media kind source: primary artifact (matches card preview).
- Media kind buckets: video / image / other.
- Prompt search fields: metadata.prompt only (not negative_prompt or other text).
- Backend impl: Python-side filter on parsed block_results JSON; no schema migration. Fine for personal-tool scale.
- URL state via search params (project convention from CLAUDE.md).

title: Multi-video generation + stitcher block
description:
Enable generating multiple videos sequentially in a single block run (Wan 2.2 already supports this via newline-separated prompts) and stitch the ordered outputs into one combined video via a new downstream Video Stitcher block.

ASK
- 'Director Mode' on Wan blocks: >1 prompts, submitted in parallel, ordered outputs preserved.
- New downstream 'Video Stitcher' block: uses ffmpeg to combine an ordered list of videos into one. Supports crossfade effects. Re-render on settings change.
- User also raised an alternative: a kitchen-sink 'Video Director' block (stitch + speed + slow + LUT).

KEY DISCOVERIES
- wan_22_image_to_video already splits prompt on newlines, submits in parallel via Promise.allSettled, polls via startNewPendingPoll which preserves submission order (serverless-poller.ts:180 — submitted.map(entry => jobMap.get(entry.idx))). Output is setOutput('video', artifacts) — an ORDERED array.
- So Director Mode is largely a UX/labeling change, not a backend change. The real new work is the stitcher block.
- PORT_VIDEO is already an array-capable port used by image_viewer/video_viewer style consumers.

DECISION TREE (in progress — to be filled during grilling)
design:
DECISIONS (from grilling 2026-05-15)

Q1 Block shape → Build BOTH a dedicated video_stitcher AND a separate video_fx block.
  Why: composable, single-purpose, matches existing convention.

Q2 ffmpeg runtime → Local FastAPI subprocess.
  Why: Wan poller already saves files locally (local_video_url). Cheap CPU work.
  Path: /api/blocks/video_stitcher/run, /api/blocks/video_fx/run.

Q3 Director Mode UX on Wan blocks → Toggle + per-prompt editor (add/remove/reorder rows).
  When OFF: existing textarea. When ON: row list, internally → string[].
  When prompt input is wired upstream: force OFF.
  Backend: no change — poller already preserves submission order (serverless-poller.ts:180).

Q4 Director Mode scope → Wan 2.2 i2v + t2v ONLY (not comfy_gen — its automation axes are a different concept).

Q5 Crossfade UI → Curated transition dropdown (None/Fade/Slide/Wipe/Circle/Pixelize) + duration slider 0.1–2.0s (default 0.5). Single transition between all pairs.

Q6 Stitch trigger / audio → Auto-stitch on first pipeline run. Manual "Re-stitch" button afterwards. Drop audio (Wan outputs are silent).

Q7 FX ops → Speed, LUT (.cube), Loop/boomerang. (No standalone Reverse; boomerang covers it.)

Q8 FX I/O → video[] → video[], order preserved, applied per-clip.

ASSUMPTIONS (no explicit answer; flagged in chat)
- LUT input: free-form path text field for v1 (no folder scanning).
- Stitched output dir: output/stitched/<timestamp>_<hash>.mp4

OUT OF SCOPE
- Per-pair transitions, audio, reverse-as-standalone, comfy_gen director mode, remote ffmpeg.

INVARIANT
- Order preservation must hold across Wan→fx→stitcher chain.
  Wan: submitted.map(e => jobMap.get(e.idx)) (serverless-poller.ts:180)
  fx: Promise.all over input array, index-assembled
  stitcher: ffmpeg filter graph references inputs positionally [0:v][1:v]…
notes:
Implemented on worktree feat+director-mode-and-video-blocks:
- custom_blocks/video_stitcher/{backend,frontend}.block.{py,tsx}
- custom_blocks/video_fx/{backend,frontend}.block.{py,tsx}
- Director Mode toggle + per-prompt editor in custom_blocks/wan_22_image_to_video/frontend.block.tsx
- _register.ts regenerated (auto via predev)
- TS build passes (npm run build).
- ffmpeg 8.0.1 available locally.
- Not yet smoke-tested end-to-end (needs a Wan run with multiple prompts + downstream stitcher); awaiting user manual test.

title: BlockFlow MIT license + public README rewrite + repo finalization
description: Add MIT LICENSE file. Rewrite README for public users (currently Aviv-internal). Verify remote_comfy_generator's existing LICENSE is MIT. Repo lives at github.com/Hearmeman24/BlockFlow per global brain page (already there but private?). Mostly admin. Needs grilling: any Hearmeman-internal content to scrub, contributor guide, support channel statement.
design:
## Resolved scope

Admin pass to make BlockFlow's repo OSS-ready at launch.

**License:** MIT (per D7). Single LICENSE file at repo root. Verify `remote_comfy_generator/LICENSE` is already MIT (per audit it has a LICENSE file; confirm contents).

**Repo:** flip `github.com/Hearmeman24/BlockFlow` from private to public at launch. Keep history. No rename.

**Support:** GitHub Issues for bugs + feature requests; GitHub Discussions enabled for questions, preset ideas, show-and-tell. Both linked from README.

**README rewrite:** the current README is Aviv-internal. Replace with public-user-oriented content:
- One-line description + screenshot/gif
- Why BlockFlow (positioning vs raw ComfyUI / vs other pipeline tools)
- Quick start: install + `uv run app.py` + setup wizard
- Feature highlights with links to /docs/
- Architecture in a paragraph (Next.js + FastAPI, RunPod-backed, ComfyGen required)
- Where to get help (Issues / Discussions)
- License

**/docs/ scaffolding:** the top-level docs directory exists; this bead delivers the index page (`/docs/README.md`) that links to all per-feature docs delivered by other beads. Also adds:
- `/docs/contributing.md` — how to file issues, propose features, the CI grep gate (cross-ref .9), the public/private block split
- `/docs/architecture.md` — high-level system diagram, request lifecycle, where data lives

## Why these calls
- MIT for max adoption (D7 already locked)
- Public-not-rename keeps existing URL recognition + history
- GH-native support (Issues + Discussions) is the cheapest functional option that doesn't require a Discord moderation effort
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] MIT LICENSE file at repo root with current year + Hearmeman24 copyright.
- [ ] Verify `remote_comfy_generator/LICENSE` is MIT; if not, address (out of this repo but flag to ComfyGen).
- [ ] README rewritten for public users: one-line pitch, screenshot/gif, quick start (install → `uv run app.py` → setup wizard), feature highlights, architecture paragraph, support links, license. Aviv-internal references removed.
- [ ] GitHub Discussions enabled on the repo with categories: General, Q&A, Show & Tell, Preset Ideas, Feature Requests.
- [ ] Issue templates added: bug report, feature request, preset proposal.
- [ ] `/docs/README.md` index page listing all per-feature docs (links to /docs/settings.md, /docs/setup-wizard.md, /docs/presets.md, /docs/lora-training.md, /docs/blocks/<each-public-block>.md, /docs/private-blocks.md, /docs/contributing.md, /docs/architecture.md).
- [ ] `/docs/contributing.md` — how to contribute, the CI grep gate from .9, the public/private block split, code style, where to discuss before opening PRs.
- [ ] `/docs/architecture.md` — high-level diagram (BlockFlow ↔ Settings ↔ ComfyGen / Trainer endpoints ↔ R2 ↔ preset registry).
- [ ] Repo visibility flipped from private to public AT LAUNCH (gated on all other v1 beads being closed).
- [ ] All other /docs/<feature>.md files delivered by their respective beads are present and link-correct before launch.

## Out of scope
- Marketing site, landing page (separate effort)
- Logo / visual identity rework
- Code-of-conduct file (can be added post-launch if community grows)
- Sponsor / funding configuration

title: comfy_gen block: preset dropdown
description: Add a preset selector to the comfy_gen block alongside the existing freeform workflow input. Selecting a preset auto-loads its workflow JSON + sets default params. Freeform mode remains for power users. Needs grilling: UI layout, what 'overriding a preset' means, can presets be edited inline, parameter exposure.
design:
## Resolved scope (post-grill)

Add a preset selector to the comfy_gen block. The block already auto-detects + surfaces common workflow params (seed, steps, resolution, etc.) from the JSON — that machinery is reused as-is. Presets are just a curated source of starter workflows; the existing param-detection layer renders the UI controls.

**UI shape:**
- Top of block: 'Preset' dropdown listing all installed presets (from .3's Settings record) + 'Custom workflow (paste JSON)' option (today's default behavior)
- Picking a preset loads its workflow.json into the block, which triggers the existing parse-workflow + param-detection pipeline → same UI flow as a user-pasted workflow, just sourced from a preset
- Edits to a loaded preset's workflow are allowed; mode label switches to "Custom (modified from <preset-name>)" so users remember what they started from
- Clicking 'Custom workflow' resets to today's freeform behavior

**No new parameter system.** Per-preset polished param UIs (manifest-declared exposed params, slider ranges, etc.) deferred to v2 once usage patterns are clearer.

## Why these calls
- Reuses every line of existing workflow-parsing and param-detection code — smallest possible v1 change for the preset paradigm
- Edit-from-preset is the natural extension of how users already interact with the block (paste, tweak, run)
- Deferring nice param UIs avoids over-designing without real usage data; manifest-driven param exposure can be added when the marketplace has enough presets to justify it
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Preset dropdown added as the first control in the comfy_gen block; lists installed presets from Settings (via .3) + a 'Custom workflow (paste JSON)' entry.
- [ ] Selecting a preset loads its workflow.json (from the local preset cache populated by .3) and triggers the existing parse-workflow + LoRA detection + param surfacing logic — no new param-detection code.
- [ ] Editing a loaded preset's workflow flips the mode label to "Custom (modified from <preset-name>)"; switching the dropdown back to that preset resets the workflow (with confirm if there are unsaved edits).
- [ ] 'Custom workflow' selection restores today's freeform paste-JSON UX.
- [ ] When no presets are installed, dropdown shows only 'Custom workflow' + an inline hint linking to the Presets page.
- [ ] When a selected preset is uninstalled (via .3's uninstall flow), block falls back to 'Custom workflow' with a notice "<preset> was uninstalled."
- [ ] Existing comfy_gen behaviors (LoRA detection, LoRA strength sliders, model download prompts, freeform workflow paste) all continue to work identically — preset path just supplies the workflow.
- [ ] `/docs/blocks/comfy-gen.md`: covers the freeform mode AND preset mode, including how param detection works across both, what 'Custom (modified from X)' means, LoRA selection UX (cross-cut from .7).

## Out of scope
- Manifest-declared exposed params with custom UI controls (deferred to v2)
- Preset editor / fork-and-save (preset stays read-only from the registry; modifications are local-only)
- Multi-preset composition in one run

title: Backend-agnostic generation blocks (Replicate/fal/external APIs)
description: Parallel track to the BlockFlow OSS push (sgs-ui-wisp-las). BlockFlow's setup wizard will be RunPod-first/deep, but users will want to plug in other backends — Replicate, fal.ai, and other hosted inference providers — for models BlockFlow doesn't ship a RunPod worker for. Scope: design generic blocks that wrap external inference APIs, with per-block credential config in BlockFlow's settings. Not blocking the OSS push; defer until after the RunPod story is stable.
